name: Trijueque and Hita from Torija — circular by Jadraque and the Castillo del Cid
name_original: Trijueque y Hita desde Torija — circular por Jadraque y el Castillo del Cid
region_intro: Touring cycling around Torija offers diverse terrain within the Alcarria region of Guadalajara, Spain. The landscape features a mix of low mountains, agricultural zones, and wooded areas, including oak and pine forests. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse quiet rural roads, olive groves, and vineyards, often alongside traditional dry stone walls. The area also includes river valleys, such as the narrow Torija valley and sections along the Henares River. Best touring cycling routes around Torija The most popular touring cycling route is Guadalajara from Torija — circular by Alarilla and the river Henares, a 47.2 miles (75.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular journey with views along the Henares River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Álvar Fáñez Route and Brihuega Loop, a difficult 34.2 miles (55.1 km) path. This route provides a loop through varied Alcarria scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Trijueque and Hita from Torija — circular by Jadraque and the Castillo del Cid, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail leading through charming villages and past historical landmarks like the Castillo del Cid, often completed in about 6 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Torija is defined by the Alcarria plateau, olive groves, and low mountains. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Torija are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 3 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Torija's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Torija?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Torija, offering a wide range of options for exploring the Alcarria region. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Torija?
A: The terrain around Torija is diverse, featuring a mix of low mountains, agricultural zones, and wooded areas with oak and pine forests. You'll cycle through picturesque landscapes with olive groves, vineyards, and traditional dry stone walls, often on quiet rural roads. Some routes also follow river valleys, such as the narrow Torija valley and sections along the Henares River.

Q: What historical landmarks or cultural sites can I see along touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer glimpses of the region's rich history. A prominent landmark is the medieval Torija Castle, which houses the Museum of "Journey to La Alcarria." You might also pass through charming villages like Hita, which is a highlight itself (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hita&lt;/a&gt;), and see the 16th-century Renaissance Parish Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción in Torija. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185622376/trijueque-and-hita-from-torija-circular-by-jadraque-and-the-castillo-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trijueque and Hita from Torija — circular by Jadraque and the Castillo del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads past historical sites.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the varied landscape of the Alcarria plateau offers several scenic spots. You can find viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Peña Hueva&lt;/a&gt;, providing expansive vistas of the surrounding countryside. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189748619/guadalajara-from-torija-circular-by-alarilla-and-the-river-henares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalajara from Torija — circular by Alarilla and the river Henares&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful views along the Henares River.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, among the 27 touring cycling routes around Torija, there are 3 routes classified as easy, making them suitable for families or beginners looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable experience for all skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Torija?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in Torija are generally spring and autumn. During these months, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder temperatures of winter. The landscapes are also particularly vibrant in spring with blooming flora and in autumn with changing leaf colors.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Torija?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Torija are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189748619/guadalajara-from-torija-circular-by-alarilla-and-the-river-henares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalajara from Torija — circular by Alarilla and the river Henares&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185622376/trijueque-and-hita-from-torija-circular-by-jadraque-and-the-castillo-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trijueque and Hita from Torija — circular by Jadraque and the Castillo del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, which offer convenient loops for exploration.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Torija?
A: While specific designated cyclist parking areas are not detailed, Torija, as a municipality, generally offers public parking options within the town. It's advisable to look for parking near the town center or close to the starting points of popular routes, such as the area around Torija Castle, which is a central landmark.

Q: Is public transport available to reach touring cycling routes in Torija?
A: Torija is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger towns like Guadalajara. While direct public transport to specific trailheads might be limited, you can typically reach Torija town center by bus and then start your cycling adventure from there. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay overnight for cyclists in the Torija area?
A: Yes, Torija and the surrounding charming villages like Valdesaz, Trijueque, and Hita offer various options for cyclists. You can find local restaurants and cafes to refuel during your ride. For overnight stays, there are accommodations ranging from guesthouses to small hotels, particularly in Torija itself, providing a comfortable base for multi-day cycling tours.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Torija?
A: The touring cycling routes around Torija are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists frequently praise the diverse terrain, which includes quiet rural roads, olive groves, and low mountains, as well as the opportunity to explore charming villages and historical landmarks like Torija Castle. The variety of routes, from moderate to difficult, also caters to different preferences.

Q: Are there any less crowded touring cycling routes around Torija?
A: The Alcarria region around Torija is known for its peaceful rural roads and tranquil landscapes, making many routes less crowded than those in more heavily touristed areas. To find particularly quiet routes, consider exploring options that venture further into the agricultural zones and wooded areas away from main villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185572381/alvar-fanez-route-and-brihuega-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Álvar Fáñez Route and Brihuega Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a journey through varied Alcarria scenery that can be quite serene.
tips: Torija Castle is an exquisite medieval military fortress and a must-see heritage site on the Camino del Cid. The castle contains a museum showcasing the history and culture of the region.

On the plateau of the Iberian system, the town of Trijueque was an outpost on the route to Torija marked today by the ruins of a castle, of which only parts of a tower remain. You must go up to the Alcarria viewpoint to get incredible views of the unique landscape of the area.

Hard to miss in Jadraque: the fortress high up on the castle hill, also called "Castillo del Cid". Its current appearance can be traced back to the 15th century, but it was probably already there as a somewhat smaller fortress during the times of the caliphate.

El Cid or Jadraque Castle is a 15th century Spanish fortress located in the municipality of Jadraque (Guadalajara). From its height, it watches over an extensive area of the Henares river plain, with the mountains of northern Guadalajara in the background.

Hita preserves its medieval old town, which was declared a historical site in 1965. Under Muslim rule, a fortress was established on top of the hill. The castle, the narrow streets and its walls are still the product of an ancient history of this pretty town! One of the most beautiful in the province!
